title: FtsH (SP0013) — membrane-anchored protease dependency under daptomycin stress
name: FtsH (SP0013) — membrane-anchored protease dependency under daptomycin stress
text: Study-derived functional-genomics record from comparative transposon insertion sequencing in Streptococcus pneumoniae strain backgrounds T4 and 19F under daptomycin stress. FtsH, locus SP0013, is a membrane-anchored ATP-dependent protease involved in removing aberrant membrane proteins and controlling membrane turnover. Its placement in the Tn-seq daptomycin-sensitivity network supports a conditionally important membrane-protein quality-control role during antibiotic-induced membrane destabilization. This record represents conditional fitness evidence and does not claim universal baseline essentiality.
description: A membrane-anchored protease linked by comparative Tn-seq to membrane-protein stability, membrane turnover and antibiotic-stress fitness.
essentiality_scope: conditionally important under the assayed stress context
provenance: van Opijnen, Dedrick and Bento, PLOS Pathogens 2016
